class FenwickTree
    def initialize(size)
        @tree = Array.new(size + 1, 0)
    end

    def update(index, delta)
        index += 1
        while index <= @tree.size
            @tree[index] += delta
            index += index & -index
        end
    end

    def query(index)
        index += 1
        sum = 0
        while index > 0
            sum += @tree[index]
            index -= index & -index
        end
        sum
    end

    def range_query(l, r)
        return query(r) - query(l - 1)
    end
end

def solve(nums, queries)
    n = nums.length
    depths = nums.map { |x| compute_depth(x) }
    bit_trees = Array.new(6) { FenwickTree.new(n) }

    depths.each_with_index do |d, i|
        if d <= 5
            bit_trees[d].update(i, 1)
        end
    end

    answer = []
    queries.each do |q|
        if q[0] == 1
            l = q[1]
            r = q[2]
            k = q[3]
            if k < 0 || k > 5
                answer << 0
                next
            end
            count = bit_trees[k].range_query(l, r)
            answer << count
        else
            idx = q[1]
            val = q[2]
            old_val = nums[idx]
            old_depth = compute_depth(old_val)
            if old_depth <= 5
                bit_trees[old_depth].update(idx, -1)
            end
            nums[idx] = val
            new_depth = compute_depth(val)
            if new_depth <= 5
                bit_trees[new_depth].update(idx, 1)
            end
        end
    end
    answer
end

def compute_depth(x)
    depth = 0
    while x != 1
        x = x.to_s(2).count('1')
        depth += 1
    end
    depth
end

    def initialize(size)

            index += index & -index

            index -= index & -index
        end

class FenwickTree
    def initialize(size)
        @tree = Array.new(size + 1, 0)
    end

    def update(index, delta)
        index += 1
        while index <= @tree.size
            @tree[index] += delta
            index += index & -index
        end
    end

    def query(index)
        index += 1
        sum = 0
        while index > 0
            sum += @tree[index]
            index -= index & -index
        end
        sum
    end

    def range_query(l, r)
        return query(r) - query(l - 1)
    end

}

impl FenwickTree {
    fn new(size: usize) -> Self {
        let mut tree = vec![0; size + 1];
        Self { tree }
    }

    fn update(&mut self, index: usize, delta: i64) {
        let mut i = index + 1;
        while i <= self.tree.len() {
            self.tree[i] += delta;
            i += i & -i;
        }
    }

    fn query(&self, index: usize) -> i64 {
        let mut sum = 0;
        let mut i = index + 1;
        while i > 0 {
            sum += self.tree[i];
            i -= i & -i;
        }
        sum
    }

    fn range_query(&self, l: usize, r: usize) -> i64 {
        self.query(r) - self.query(l - 1)
    }

fn solve(nums: Vec<i64>, queries: Vec<Vec<i64>>) -> Vec<i64> {
    let n = nums.len();
    let mut bit_trees: Vec<FenwickTree> = Vec::new();
    for _ in 0..6 {
        bit_trees.push(FenwickTree::new(n));
    }

    for i in 0..n {
        let d = compute_depth(nums[i]);
        if d <= 5 {
            bit_trees[d as usize].update(i, 1);
        }
    }

    let mut answer = Vec::new();
    for q in queries {
        if q[0] == 1 {
            let l = q[1] as usize;
            let r = q[2] as usize;
            let k = q[3] as usize;
            if k < 0 || k > 5 {
                answer.push(0);
                continue;
            }
            let count = bit_trees[k].range_query(l, r);
            answer.push(count);
        } else {
            let idx = q[1] as usize;
            let val = q[2];
            let old_val = nums[idx];
            let old_depth = compute_depth(old_val);
            if old_depth <= 5 {
                bit_trees[old_depth as usize].update(idx, -1);
            }
            nums[idx] = val;
            let new_depth = compute_depth(val);
            if new_depth <= 5 {
                bit_trees[new_depth as usize].update(idx, 1);
            }
        }
    }

    answer
}

struct FenwickTree {
    tree: Vec<i64>,
}

impl FenwickTree {
    fn new(size: usize) -> Self {
        let mut tree = vec![0; size + 1];
        Self { tree }
    }

    fn update(&mut self, index: usize, delta: i64) {
        let index = index + 1; // 1-based
        let mut i = index;
        while i <= self.tree.len() {
            self.tree[i] += delta;
            i += i & -i;
        }
    }

    fn query(&self, index: usize) -> i64 {
        let mut sum = 0;
        let mut i = index + 1; // 1-based
        while i > 0 {
            sum += self.tree[i];
            i -= i & -i;
        }
        sum
    }

    fn range_query(&self, l: usize, r: usize) -> i64 {
        self.query(r) - self.query(l - 1)
    }
}
